free_text: deuteron induced nuclear reactions on natural Mo up
Natural Mo metalic high purity foils and Mo/Re alloy
Results from nine series of experiments are reported.
Stacks containing Mo or Mo/Re targets and Al and Ti
monitor foils were irradiated at low beam intensities
The activity was measured without chemical separation
The effective energy for each foil was determined
using the energy-range formula and tables of Andersen
and Ziegler, and corrected by comparing the
simultaneously re-measured cross sections of the
monitor reactions to the latest recommended values.
The activities were determined from the photo-peak
areas, taking into account corrections for decay
during irradiation, waiting and measuring time.
Decay data and the Q-values of the were taken from
NUDAT and from Q-value calculator of NNDC.
The uncertainty of the beam energy in the
middle of each irradiated foil was estimated
combining the uncertainties of the energy degradation
depending on the thickness of the target foils in the
The uncertainty of the cross-sections were
estimated taking the independent relative errors of the
linearly contributing components, number of the
bombarding particles, number of the target nuclei,
decay data, detector efficiency and peak area were
summed quadratically, and the square root of the sum
was taken. The uncertainties of the cross-sections were
Fitted excitation functions were used for
